concepts.title: Indus Valley Civilization was a major urban civilization
concepts.description: The Indus Valley Civilization was a major urban civilization that existed in the northwestern regions of South Asia, present-day Pakistan and northwestern India. It is considered one of the earliest urban civilizations in the world.

concepts.title: The Early Harappans developed a sophisticated urban civilization
concepts.description: The Early Harappans developed a sophisticated urban civilization with well-planned cities, advanced irrigation systems, and a system of weights and measures.

concepts.title: The Indus Valley Civilization had a writing system
concepts.description: The Indus Valley Civilization had a writing system, which has not yet been deciphered, and is considered one of the most enigmatic aspects of the civilization.

concepts.title: The cities of Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa were the largest cities of the Indus Valley Civilization
concepts.description: The cities of Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa were the largest cities of the Indus Valley Civilization, with well-planned streets, public baths, and sewage systems.

concepts.title: Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa were the Twin Cities of the Indus Valley Civilization
concepts.description: Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa were the Twin Cities of the Indus Valley Civilization. Use the mnemonic 'M and H are Twin Cities' to remember this fact.

concepts.title: The Indus Valley Civilization was a nomadic civilization
concepts.description: The Indus Valley Civilization was a settled, urban civilization, not a nomadic one. Students should be aware of this common misconception and understand the significance of urbanization in the civilization.

concepts.title: The Indus Valley Civilization had a system of weights and measures
concepts.description: The Indus Valley Civilization had a system of weights and measures, which was one of the earliest known systems in the world.

concepts.title: Key Features of the Indus Valley Civilization
concepts.description: Key Features of the Indus Valley Civilization include: urbanization, sophisticated urban planning, advanced irrigation systems, system of weights and measures, and a writing system.

concepts.title: The Indus Valley Civilization declined due to environmental degradation
concepts.description: The Indus Valley Civilization declined due to environmental degradation, including the drying up of the Indus River and the collapse of the regional economy.
